name: Mobile capture → knowledge folder
_comment: SKELETON — finish/verify in the n8n editor (needs a real Telegram bot token + the local model running). Flow: poll Telegram getUpdates (no inbound) → allowlist-guard your chat id → for each shared URL, fetch it → extract readable text → summarise via the LOCAL model → write a Notesage-frontmatter .md into the mounted capture folder (/capture, = $CAPTURE_DIR on the host). THREE things to finish in the editor are marked TODO below: (a) getUpdates offset tracking via workflow static data, (b) robust HTML→text extraction, (c) text→file conversion before the write node.

nodes.name: Telegram getUpdates (poll)
nodes._comment: TODO(a): pass ?offset=<last_update_id+1> from workflow static data so messages aren't reprocessed.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'local', messages: [ { role: 'system', content: 'Summarise the page in 5 lines; first line is a concise title.' }, { role: 'user', content: $json.text } ], max_tokens: 400 }) }}

nodes.name: Summarise (local model)
nodes._comment: LLAMA_BASE_URL = the in-container URL to reach llama.cpp via the host gateway bridge, e.g. http://192.168.64.1:8080/v1

nodes.name: Write to capture folder
nodes._comment: TODO(c): readWriteFile writes a BINARY property — add a 'Convert to File'/'Move Binary Data' step turning $json.md into binary 'data' before this node. Writes into /capture, which is $CAPTURE_DIR mounted from the host.
